ideas.name: funding_settlement_rebalance_drift
ideas.mechanism: Perpetual funding settles every 8h at 00:00/08:00/16:00 UTC. When funding has been persistently one-signed and large across the preceding interval, leveraged carry desks and delta-neutral basis funds face a mechanical cash flow AT settlement that they hedge in spot: longs paying heavy funding trim, and the systematic basis-arb book rebalances its spot leg. This is not a coil breaking at a clock (the 0-for-4 dead lineage) — it is a recurring forced cash-flow event whose DIRECTION is set by the accumulated funding sign, which we proxy from OHLCV as a persistent multi-bar directional drift with compressing range into the settlement bar (crowding). The residual is un-arbitraged at 5-min retail scale because the flow is spread across the post-settlement hour by execution algos to minimize impact, leaving a slow directional bleed rather than an instant repricing that HFT would capture. We fade the crowded side: heavy same-signed pre-settlement drift means the carry crowd is offside and the settlement forces the unwind.
ideas.lineage_check: Nearest death: options_expiry_pin_release (r002) and asian_to_london_handoff_thrust (r003), both named-time COIL-BREAK deaths where a clock lent no directional energy to a range break. This is categorically different: it does not trade a range break, it trades an accumulated-drift UNWIND driven by a real recurring cash-flow event (funding settlement), and its direction is set by the drift sign (contrarian to crowding), not by the break direction. Also distinct from round_number lineage — no price level involved.
ideas.entry_rule: At each 8h settlement boundary bar (00:00, 08:00, 16:00 UTC), look back over the prior 96 bars (8h). Compute cumulative signed return R_pre and count fraction of those bars whose sign matches sign(R_pre) = persistence P. Compute range compression C = ATR(last 24 bars) / ATR(bars 48-96 ago). Qualify if |R_pre| >= 1.5% AND P >= 0.60 AND C <= 0.75 (crowded, compressing drift). Enter SHORT if R_pre > 0 (fade the crowded longs being unwound), LONG if R_pre < 0. Enter on the settlement boundary bar close. One symbol per fire; respect 1h cooldown.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: 3 settlements/day x 5 symbols = 15 boundary evaluations/day. Premise gates: |R_pre|>=1.5% over 8h holds ~35% of the time in this basket (measured drift is common); P>=0.60 given |R_pre|>=1.5% is positively correlated (a large move IS made of same-signed bars) ~55%; C<=0.75 compression is roughly independent of drift sign and holds ~30%. These are NOT anti-correlated (large drift and same-sign persistence co-occur by construction). Joint ~ 0.35 x 0.55 x 0.30 = 0.058 per evaluation. 15 x 0.058 = 0.87 fires/day raw. After 1h cooldown (settlements are 8h apart, no collision) and slot occupancy (24h hold, ~3.6 concurrent-symbol capacity across 5 slots is comfortable at <1 fire/day), place ~0.6/day net. Over 930 days: ~560 placed. Comfortably > 100.
ideas.fee_survival: The fade targets a forced-unwind glide of a crowded 8h drift; base rate of such reversions moving >1% within 24h against a 1.5%+ crowded position is plausible because the crowd's own accumulated drift is the fuel. The 5%/3% asymmetric exit is the risk: a fade against continuation dies (FADE-SHAPED lesson). This survives ONLY if the settlement is a genuine directional trigger — that is the falsifiable claim. Volatility-conditional slippage is mild here because settlement flow is algo-spread across the hour, not a shock.
ideas.kill_criterion: Net expectancy < +0.5%/trade over the full window at n>=100, OR win rate < 34% (below the +5/-3 breakeven), decisively kills it. Given the FADE-SHAPED prior, a win rate at or below 34% confirms settlement carries no directional energy — the fifth calendar death.

ideas.name: constraint_saturation_capacity_gate_shock_fade
ideas.mechanism: NEW information channel per the family-closure clause: this gate conditions NOT on the placebo arm's or portfolio's own TRADE OUTCOMES (the closed self-referential-gate family) but on the CONSTRAINT LAYER'S REJECTION STATE — how many candidate signals across all 5 symbols were rejected by the 5-slot/cooldown/$1k ceiling in the recent window. When rejection saturation is HIGH, many symbols are simultaneously generating admissible signals — a market-wide broad-participation state. This is external information no single engine's trade stream contains: an engine sees only its own fills, never the count of things the constraint layer turned away. High cross-symbol admission pressure marks a synchronized-volatility regime; in such regimes the measured multi-day shock-fade (the -3 to -4% contrarian effect) is strongest because forced deleveraging is basket-wide. The gate AMPLIFIES the shock-fade engine by firing it only when the whole basket is under coordinated stress.
ideas.lineage_check: The self-referential-gate family (drawdown_regime_contrarian_gate r001 ... placebo_streak_gated_weekly_trend_engine r005) is CLOSED — all five conditioned on the system's own trade OUTCOMES (null-arm win rate, losing streaks, stop-out clusters). This gate conditions on the ADMISSION/REJECTION count of the constraint layer, a distinct channel that carries cross-symbol synchronization information absent from any engine's outcome stream — the specific new mechanism the closure clause demands. Kill_criterion registers the ungated control as a second variant per the r006/r007 lesson.
ideas.entry_rule: Maintain a rolling rejection counter: over the trailing 24h (288 bars), count how many bars across ALL 5 symbols produced a qualifying shock-fade candidate (defined below) that the constraint layer could NOT place (slots full or cooldown active). Gate is OPEN when this rejected-candidate count in the trailing 24h >= 4 (basket-wide saturation). ENGINE (shock-fade): a symbol prints a bar with |return| >= 3% AND volume >= 3x median(20). Enter CONTRARIAN to that bar (fade) at next bar open. Only fire the engine when the gate is OPEN. REGISTER a second variant `constraint_saturation_shock_fade_UNGATED` identical but with the gate always open, for the matched control.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: Raw shock-fade candidates: 3%+/3x-vol bars fire ~0.4/sym/day (measured shock population n=53 full-pop over prior probes scales up at this looser 3x threshold to ~0.4). 5 symbols x 930 days x 0.4 = ~1860 raw candidates. Gate-open fraction: rejection>=4 in trailing 24h is a MEAN-based single threshold, not a rare conjunction — during synchronized-vol clusters (which the shock-fade population concentrates in), roughly 40% of shock candidates occur while the basket is already saturated. 1860 x 0.40 = ~744 gate-open candidates. After 72h slot occupancy (occupies a slot 3 days; 5 slots support ~1.7 concurrent, and clustered fires collide) place ~25%: ~186. Ungated control places ~450. Both > 100.
ideas.fee_survival: 72h hold targeting a 6% contrarian move engages the measured -3 to -4% multi-day shock-fade directly, amortizing the 0.6% floor over a multi-day resolution (10x better cost ratio than 24h theses). The gate concentrates fires into basket-wide-stress windows where the deleveraging fade is largest. Slippage spikes at shocks — the 6% target and multi-day hold absorb it.
ideas.kill_criterion: DECISIVE: median (gated-minus-ungated) net expectancy delta <= 0 at n>=100 both arms — the gate carries no capacity information, matching the closed family. FALLBACK: gated net expectancy < +0.5%/trade kills the spec absolutely. Both variants registered in this round's gauntlet so the matched control actually runs.

ideas.name: multiday_shock_fade_contrarian_hold
ideas.mechanism: The measured control arm (2026-07-19) found sign-aligned forward returns after 3%/3x-vol shock bars are -3.0% at 24h, -4.3% at 72h, -3.3% at 120h across n=53 full population — the largest gross effect ever measured in this project, and it is CONTRARIAN. The mechanism is forced deleveraging: a 3%+ shock triggers liquidation cascades that overshoot fundamental value, and over the following 2-5 days the overshoot mean-reverts as liquidity replenishes and the forced sellers/buyers are exhausted. This is un-arbitraged at retail scale because the reversion is slow (days) and noisy (violent retests), too gradual for HFT and too painful for discretionary traders to hold through the -3% intraday swings. We take the contrarian side of the shock and HOLD for the multi-day reversion, sized so the asymmetric exit MATCHES the reversion thesis (wide stop, wide target, days).
ideas.lineage_check: Nearest death: post_shock_multiday_drift (r003), which traded shock direction as CONTINUATION and died — but the correction (2026-07-19) OVERTURNED its mechanism: the drift is real and CONTRARIAN. This idea trades the opposite sign, the direction the control arm actually measured positive. Also distinct from the closed shock/point-process import family (Omori/R0/Hawkes) which all chased the impulse; this fades it over days. Exit shape matches thesis (reversion under a symmetric-enough exit), avoiding the exit-confound self-sabotage.
ideas.entry_rule: A symbol prints a shock bar: |bar return| >= 3.5% AND volume >= 3x median(volume, 20 bars) AND the bar's close is within 25% of its extreme (a true thrust, not a reversal wick). Enter CONTRARIAN at next bar open: if shock bar was up, enter SHORT; if down, enter LONG. Hold with wide multi-day exit. One fire per symbol per shock; 1h cooldown.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: 3.5%+/3x-vol shock bars with close near extreme: the raw 3% shock fires ~0.4/sym/day; tightening to 3.5% and requiring close-near-extreme (positively correlated with a large-body thrust, ~70% of large shocks close near extreme) gives ~0.25/sym/day. 5 symbols x 930 days x 0.25 = ~1163 raw. Slot occupancy at 96h (4 days per slot; 5 slots = ~1.25 concurrent capacity; clustered shocks collide heavily) places ~15%: ~174. Above 100.
ideas.fee_survival: Targets a 10% contrarian reversion over 96h — the measured effect is -4.3% sign-aligned at 72h, i.e. ~+4% in our favor as a base, with the tail reaching the 10% target on full mean-reversion moves. The 0.6% floor is a rounding error against a 10% target at a 4-day horizon (~15x cost ratio). Slippage spikes AT the shock entry — mitigated by entering at next-bar open (post-cascade, spread partially recovered) and a wide 6% stop absorbing the volatile retest.
ideas.kill_criterion: Net expectancy < +0.5%/trade at n>=100 kills it — this directly tests whether the measured contrarian effect survives real fees and slippage and the +10/-6 exit. If it dies, the largest gross effect measured here does not survive execution, closing the shock-fade band.

ideas.name: post_shock_calm_reentry_maker_hold
ideas.mechanism: Attacks the denominator by entering where price comes to us WITHOUT adverse selection — the confirmed trap in pullback-to-breakout (r003) and retest-adverse-selection studies is that waiting at a breakout level selects failures. We invert the selection: after a large shock and a 12-24h CALM (range contraction, no new shock), the market has stabilized and a resting maker limit placed slightly INTO the direction of the measured multi-day reversion gets filled by ordinary post-shock chop, NOT by a failing breakout's retrace. The population is 'shock followed by calm' — waiting here is NOT adverse selection because we are not waiting at a level a trend must fail to reach; we are waiting in a stabilized post-shock band where reversion drift brings price to a maker limit on the far side of the shock. Maker execution roughly halves the round-trip cost (~0.3% vs 0.6%), directly amortizing the floor. Un-arbitraged because the fill requires patience through a multi-day hold most retail cannot stomach.
ideas.lineage_check: Nearest death: pullback_to_breakout_level_limit (r003) and weekly_pullback_limit_into_uptrend (r005, starved 186x) — both placed resting limits that selected failures OR never touched. This differs on both counts: (1) the population is post-shock CALM, not a breakout retest, so waiting selects reversion not failure (retest adverse-selection study measured +0.98% for never-retested vs -0.89% retested — we sit in the never-adverse post-shock band); (2) base rate is derived from the frequent shock population, not a rare touchable pullback inside a slow regime (the 166x/186x rare-touch trap). The touch here is common post-shock chop, not a rare level-inside-a-regime coincidence.
ideas.entry_rule: A symbol printed a shock bar (|return| >= 3% AND volume >= 3x median(20)) at some point in the prior 24h. THEN require CALM confirmation: the most recent 24 bars (2h) show ATR(24) <= 0.6 x ATR-during-shock-window (contraction) AND no new 2%+ bar. Place a resting maker LIMIT order 1.0% on the CONTRARIAN side of the current price (into the measured reversion direction: if shock was up, limit BELOW; if down, limit ABOVE — buying/selling the post-shock dip toward reversion). Order rests up to 12h; if unfilled, cancel (no trade). On fill, hold with multi-day exit. 1h cooldown per symbol.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: Shock bars fire ~0.4/sym/day; the 'shock in prior 24h + calm confirmation' population: calm follows shocks ~50% of the time within 24h (the other half retests violently), so ~0.2 calm-post-shock setups/sym/day. Maker limit 1% away fills within 12h during post-shock chop ~55% of the time (ordinary volatility bridges 1% readily in calm-but-still-elevated post-shock tape). 0.2 x 0.55 = 0.11 filled/sym/day. 5 x 930 x 0.11 = ~511 raw fills. Slot occupancy at 96h places ~30% (calm setups less clustered than shocks): ~153. Above 100.
ideas.fee_survival: Maker fill halves cost to ~0.3% round-trip; the 1% limit offset is itself a favorable entry (we enter 1% better than market into the reversion). Targeting 8% over 96h against the measured -4% multi-day reversion gives a ~13x cost ratio. The maker fill is the design's core cost-attack, and the post-shock-calm population is the answer to the adverse-selection trap.
ideas.kill_criterion: Net expectancy (maker fill model) < +0.5%/trade at n>=100 kills it. Additionally, if the filled population's win rate is INDISTINGUISHABLE from or worse than the always-market-enter version, the maker fill is selecting failures (adverse selection re-confirmed) — decisive.

ideas.name: directional_weekly_momentum_hold_canary
ideas.mechanism: MANDATED CONTINUATION CANARY. The regime-split grid (2026-07-20) measured that DIRECTIONAL monthly momentum earned +13-29%/hold in 2020-2023 then FLIPPED to -12.4% in 2024-25 (our archive). The 2-week TSMOM band is the ONE place directional momentum previously BEAT drift and marks the weekly-to-monthly crossover as OPEN. This engine is explicitly designed to be the regime alarm: a directional (long AND short) weekly-momentum entry held multi-day. If continuation is dead (current epoch), it fails informatively; if the regime has FLIPPED BACK to trending, this is the first idea to catch it — the load-bearing canary. The 'gate' pairing: fire it only when the basket shows broad directional participation (multiple symbols same-signed weekly), the synchronized-trend state where a real regime is most detectable. This is a directional bet, drift-free, so it measures SIGNAL not drift-timing (the regime-split lesson's correction).
ideas.lineage_check: Nearest deaths: multiweek_directional_regime_persistence_hold (r006) and trailing_return_rank_persistence_hold (r005), both directional momentum holds that died NEGATIVE — confirming the reversion epoch. This idea is NOT a descendant expecting to WIN; it is the pre-registered canary whose NEGATIVE result confirms the epoch and whose POSITIVE result is the regime-flip alarm (CANARY MANDATE). It differs from r006 by using a SHORTER 2-week (weekly) lookback — the crossover band the grid left explicitly open — and adds a cross-sectional synchronization gate absent from r006's univariate |R14| threshold.
ideas.entry_rule: Weekly momentum: compute R_7d = 7-day (2016-bar) return per symbol. Cross-sectional gate: count symbols with |R_7d| >= 8%; gate OPEN when >= 3 symbols qualify AND the qualifying symbols are majority same-signed (synchronized trend). When gate open, for EACH qualifying symbol enter in the DIRECTION of its R_7d (long if R_7d>0, short if R_7d<0) at next bar open. Multi-day hold. 1h cooldown; re-evaluate weekly momentum each bar but one open position per symbol.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: |R_7d|>=8% holds for a given symbol ~35% of days in this volatile basket (measured qualification bands 47-71% at 6% threshold in r006; 8% gives ~35%). Cross-sectional gate (>=3 of 5 same-signed with |R|>=8%): crypto is highly synchronized, so given any symbol qualifies, >=3 co-qualify ~60% of the time. Effective per-symbol fire ~0.35 x 0.60 = 0.21/day, but with 168h (7-day) hold occupying a slot the entire hold, a symbol re-fires only every ~7 days = ~0.14 fires/sym/day cap. 5 x 930 x 0.14 = ~651 candidate fires; slot occupancy at 168h (7 days/slot, 5 slots = ~5 concurrent, tight but synchronized fires want all 5) places ~30%: ~195. Above 100.
ideas.fee_survival: Targets 12% over 168h (7 days) — a ~20x cost ratio, the fee floor is negligible. Survival depends ENTIRELY on the regime: if trending returns, 12% weekly-momentum continuation moves are common in the basket; if reversion holds, it dies as the canary predicts. This is the admissible slow-band territory with a directional (not drift-confounded) measurement.
ideas.kill_criterion: Net expectancy < 0 AND win rate < 40% at n>=100 confirms the reversion epoch persists (canary negative — expected). A POSITIVE net expectancy > +1%/trade at n>=100 is the REGIME-FLIP ALARM: continuation has returned, and this result is load-bearing, not just a pass. Either outcome is informative by design.

round_thesis: This round abandons every dead premise-shape empirically. The behavioral_calendar and meta_self_referential lenses are both closed by prior epitaphs — so I do NOT retest a range-break-at-a-clock (0-for-4) or a self-referential-gate conditioning on its own past outcomes (family CLOSED, no new mechanism named). Instead: behavioral_calendar gets a FUNDING-SETTLEMENT flow mechanism (a real institutional cash-flow event at fixed 8h boundaries carrying forced-rebalance pressure, not a coil), and meta_self_referential gets the one legal move — a gate carrying information an ungated stream cannot: cross-sectional constraint-REJECTION saturation as a portfolio-capacity signal, which conditions on the system's own admission-layer state (never on trade outcomes), a genuinely new information channel per the closure clause. multi_day_horizon and cost_engineering commit fully to the two measured open bands: the CONTRARIAN multi-day shock-fade (the largest gross effect ever measured here, -3 to -4% sign-aligned) and maker-execution at slow horizons from a NON-adverse-selection population. gate_engine_pairing carries the mandatory continuation canary — a directional weekly-momentum engine — so a regime flip announces itself in our results.
